title: Microsoft Graph SharePoint - Site, List, Drive, And CRUD Conventions
applies_to: SharePoint Online access through Microsoft Graph
Site discovery plus list, file, and folder CRUD workflows

confirmed_facts.fact: The SharePoint API in Microsoft Graph supports access to sites, lists, and drives, provides read-only support for site resources, and read-write support for lists, listItems, driveItems, and tenant-level SharePoint settings.
The SharePoint API does not support creating new sites through Microsoft Graph.
Sites can be addressed by SharePoint ID, hostname plus server-relative path, or URL-derived path forms such as /sites/{hostname}:/{relative-path}.
Tenant site discovery supports both free-text search with GET /sites?search={query} and broad enumeration with GET /sites/getAllSites.
Subsites can be enumerated with GET /sites/{site-id}/sites.
Document libraries are exposed as drives under a site, and files or folders are represented as driveItems.
SharePoint list items can be represented as listItems, and items in document libraries can be represented either as listItems or driveItems.
Files up to 250 MB can be uploaded in a single PUT to the driveItem content endpoint, while larger uploads should use createUploadSession.
Deleting a driveItem through the standard delete API moves the item to the recycle bin instead of permanently deleting it.
SharePoint lists can be created through POST /sites/{site-id}/lists, while list items can be created, updated, listed, and deleted through the list item APIs.

implementation_notes: For site discovery, choose between path-based resolution, free-text search, or tenant-wide enumeration depending on whether the caller already knows the site path.
For tabular SharePoint business data, prefer lists and listItems. For documents and folders, prefer drives and driveItems.
Normalize around site ID plus drive ID or list ID after discovery so downstream CRUD flows do not repeatedly resolve by path.
Use $select and $expand aggressively on SharePoint resources because references are hidden by default and the docs recommend selecting only the data you need.
Use document-library driveItems for files and folders even though document-library entries can also appear as listItems.
caveats: The Graph SharePoint surface does not create sites. Site provisioning must use another supported mechanism.
Permission requirements vary materially across discovery, list CRUD, and file CRUD. Keep least-privilege scopes tied to the actual operation.
List schemas are site-specific; field internal names must be discovered per list before writing item data.
